chart.title: Per capita electricity generation from fossil fuels, nuclear and renewables
chart.subtitle: Per capita electricity generation from fossil fuels (coal, gas, and oil), nuclear, and renewables (solar, wind, hydropower, bioenergy, geothermal, wave, and tidal).
chart.citation: Ember (2026); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025); Population based on various sources (2024)

Fossil fuel electricity per capita - kWh.titleShort: Electricity generation from fossil fuels per person
Fossil fuel electricity per capita - kWh.titleLong: Electricity generation from fossil fuels per person - Ember
Fossil fuel electricity per capita - kWh.descriptionShort: Electricity generation from coal, oil, and gas, measured in kilowatt-hours per person.
descriptionKey: Per capita figures are calculated by dividing total values by the population of the country or region. Population data is constructed by Our World in Data, based on [various sources](https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/population).
Fossil fuel electricity per capita - kWh.descriptionProcessing: - Electricity data from 2000 onwards (and from 1990 onwards for European countries, including Turkey) comes from Ember. Earlier data comes from the Energy Institute.
Fossil fuel electricity per capita - kWh.citationShort: Ember (2026); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025); Population based on various sources (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data
Fossil fuel electricity per capita - kWh.citationLong: Ember (2026); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2025); Population based on various sources (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data. “Electricity generation from fossil fuels per person – Ember” [dataset]. Ember, “Yearly Electricity Data Europe”; Ember, “Yearly Electricity Data”; Energy Institute, “Statistical Review of World Energy”; Various sources, “Population” [original data].
